description: Declarative two-axis mode registry for the sk-code parent hub. Every packet is a modes[] entry carrying the discriminator (workflowMode/packetKind/backendKind) plus its advisorRouting contract. The WORKFLOW axis (quality/code-review) is process; the SURFACE axis (code-webflow/code-opencode) is read-only domain evidence. Implement/debug/verify phase doctrine is consolidated in shared/references/workflow_*.md, symlinked into each surface. The advisor routes the single identity sk-code; the hub resolves the workflow mode and bundles surface packets as evidence.

discriminator.workflowMode: Public hub/mode key. Stable identity used by the hub router and this registry. Carried by every mode.
discriminator.packetKind: The axis discriminator: 'workflow' for process modes that act, 'surface' for read-only domain-evidence packets bundled alongside a workflow mode.
discriminator.backendKind: Which backend runs the mode: surface-router for surface-aware code workflows, review-cache for review output caching, evidence-base for read-only surface packets.

advisorRoutingContract.routingClass: How the advisor finds the mode. For sk-code every mode is 'metadata' = resolved by hub membership; the advisor routes the single identity sk-code and the hub picks the mode. Surface packets are advisor-invisible and reached only by hub bundling.
advisorRoutingContract.packetSkillName: The packet's SKILL.md name. Folder equals packetSkillName for every mode.
advisorRoutingContract.grandfatheredFolderMismatch: false for every mode because folder equals packetSkillName.

surface-axis.description: Declares the SURFACE axis. packetKind:'surface' entries are read-only domain-evidence packets (backendKind:'evidence-base', routingClass:'metadata', mutatesWorkspace:false) bundled alongside a workflow mode via routerPolicy.outcomes.surfaceBundle. They bundle stack knowledge plus the workflow doctrine as read-only evidence; the acting agent applies it — the surface never executes the process itself.

toolSurface.writeScopeNote: Write is scoped to the ephemeral, untracked review cache (.opencode/.code-review-cache/<repo-ref>.jsonl); code-review never writes tracked project files (Edit is forbidden), so the tracked workspace is not mutated.
